@saricubra2867

replies.text:
​@saricubra2867It's not necessarily one or the other. It can be both and more. After all Luke would have died if his actions in sparing his father inspired Anakin into action to save him from Palpatine.

We saw how flawed the Jedi were in the prequels. Removing attachments leads to secret relationships and ultimately leads to disaster as we saw with Anakin and Padme. Removing attachments does not work at all.

Being selfless helping others or being selfish because of being attached to things?

Having a life with loved ones while maintaining selflessness with everything else is more efficient imo.

replies.author: @SilvidGossamer
replies.text:
@HouseOfTheHybrid _TPM_ suffers from weak dialogue and some bad delivery thereof*, and Jar-Jar Binks (and to a lesser extent, Boss Nass) constitutes a very… _questionable_ addition to the film, but narratively and choreographically it is leaps and bounds ahead of _TLJ._

*Neeson, McGregor, McDiarmid, Park, & Secombe are all fantastic though imho; Portman, August, Quarshie, Oz, Jackson, Daniels, & Davies are all varying levels of middling

replies.author: @HouseOfTheHybrid
replies.text:
​​@SilvidGossamer In addition to the boat load of flaws you laid out it is also objectively a boring film! I do not have it over Last Jedi. People bitch about the Luke portrayal and let that cause them to overlook everything else the film did well after the fact.

My list for Skywalker saga is...
Revenge of the Sith
A New Hope
Empire Strikes Back
Force Awakens
Last Jedi
Attack of the Clones
Return of the Jedi
Phantom Menace
Rise of Skywalker

And the top 3 are the only ones I'd rank as S tier films. There is a drop off after that as the rest are either just good enough or mediocre.

replies.author: @phonepunk7888
replies.text: TLJ is way worse than Rise. At least there you have Palpatine getting one last good evil cackle. I've seen Chris defend that "But there was so much potential" and I entirely disagree. Kylo Ren losing two films in a row does not leave him as a good choice for final main villain to lose again a 3rd time. TLJ ruined the franchise completely and left them with no villain.

replies.author: @SilvidGossamer
replies.text: @HouseOfTheHybrid I don't know about objectively—this is the first I've heard anyone refer to it as boring. Is that the consensus you've garnered? I may have unknowing only come across particularly insular views (ranging anywhere from 'good' to 'bad' with respect to the film) that regard the film as whatever else, compelling and engaging—views that might stand apart from the general or perhaps critical consensus. At least in terms of personal subjectivity, I've found the film to keep my attention the whole way through.

replies.author: @Spongemonkey26

replies.author: @John-fk2ky
replies.text: @HouseOfTheHybrid you’re hurting your credibility and any claim at being able analyze anything accurately when you say that. There’s nothing wrong with The Phantom Menace as a film. It’s paced decently. Most of the dialogue isn’t bad. The story is solid. The only real problem is how well you can tolerate Jar Jar, and that’s subjective. On the other hand, the Last Jedi is boring and is a narrative mess. The Rise of Skywalker is less coherent than a clip show. That you put The Phantom Menace alongside actual garbage just makes you sound ridiculous.

replies.author: @davfree9732
replies.text:
TLJ is the worst SW film, but it's not 'the' worst film. For that you have to look at TFA.
When you break it down it doesn't tell a story. It sets up a lot of potential story, but it never makes anything concrete. It's a movie of 'suggestions' and because the plot points were suggestions it allowed RJ to shove them aside and make his film.

Basically, RJ's problem is that his instincts were bad, but he did assemble a film that had a plot.
JJ's problem is that his instincts were built upon what would be interesting, but he never pursued what the fans would have found interesting. So in a strange sense RJ is a better director than JJ because RJ will put his stamp on a film. JJ never put any identity into TFA's. That's what allowed TLJ to be so bad. TFA didn't push RJ down defined narratives that had to happen. He gave RJ the ability to discard his work. That's why TFA is the worst film while TLJ is the worst SW film.

author: @DoubleSkunkFunk
text: The figure of 593.7 million US dollars cited in the article as production costs is incorrect. According to a Forbes report from 13 October 2025, the film’s total costs were 582.3 million US dollars. Disney received a rebate of 103.8 million US dollars through UK tax incentives, reducing the net cost to 478.5 million US dollars.

author: @aaronh.917
text: I’m one of the fans that was not fooled by the force awakens. Disney messed up the minute that they jumped ahead in the timeline to a point that really took the franchise BACK to the start of a new hope. “Small group of rebels must fight off a tyrannical empire!” … yeah that happened already. Why not start up with Luke being OP and training other Jedi, then show his downfall/death and have super Rey (the awesomest Jedi evar) take the helm as the lead Jedi. Rey could have been a great “grand master Jedi” but at least give Luke his time to shine in that role first. And don’t start off with Rey being over powered in her first movie. “Luke is missing!” … why? Does that make any sense? Turns out, it didn’t.

author: @rossb.7471
text: It's impossible to calculate how much they really lost with Last Jedi and Rise of Skywalker. Because these movies derailed the entire intellectual property. If they had been successful, they could have continued to make at least one or two Star Wars movies per year, and made at least $1-1.5 billion per movie. Hypothetically, if they didn't ruin the franchise, they could have made 10-12 addional movies by now, grossing at least a billion each. So really these failures cost them upwards of $10 billion dollars.

author: @DrBovdin
text:
I used to be a great fan of SW. I watched some of the Disney films, but it careened so badly downhill that I first watched their “Episode VIII” on an intercontinental flight, and I have never seen their “Rise of Skywalker”. 
That said, I did enjoy the Andor arc , and I am keen on seeing the second season of the series. And I am also slightly interested in at some point seeing the Mandalorean, at least the first two instalments. 
I still have a small glimmer of hope that someone will resurrect the zombie at some point in the not too distant future, but I don’t place much hope in Disney unless they change dramatically.

author: @BobSurce
text:
Let's not forget how Disney shat the bed with Marvel and Indiana Jones, too.

I was excited for The Force Awakens, because of how well Disney had done with Marvel up until 2011.

Then they started their dung-ball on dung mountain which rolled down and created a Raiders of the Lost Arc sized turd at the bottom!

Why would ANYONE invest in Disney over the past 8 years?!  They like losing?

Reeee-anne Johnson made Luke drink blue soy milk in the Last Jedi.  Krapleen Kennedy egged him on and the absolute worst part of it all... Mark Hammil joined their dark side.

Aa far as I'm concerned, George Lucas Star Wars is the only Star Wars that exists.  I still watch Episodes I - VI, and still love them!

Long live original Han, Luke, Leia, Lando, Vader, Chewy 3P0, R2 Yoda and Boba!

I steal the scenes I enjoyed to keep as my own while considering the entire Disney trilogy non-canon. We all know it's not canon to George. He had another plan, in which Han, Luke and Leia survives, Chewbacca heroically dies, and there's the twin children of Han and Leia's.

replies.author: @Captain-Thievius
replies.text:
JJ doesn’t get a defense, he brought this on himself. His entire career in Hollywood was being a shyster selling people on his Mystery Box BS. 
He pitches an idea for a show or movie that is intentionally vague and not fully developed. Once he scores the pitch and gets his money, JJ departs asap leaving everyone else holding the bag.
This is how Lost and The Disney Trilogy became nonsensical and convoluted parodies of themselves. Because even the people that took over had no idea what to do once they realized that there was nothing in that Mystery Box JJ sold them.

The Rise of Palpatine was the first time JJ actually had to finish what he started, and he proved to everyone how much of a fraud he truly is. That’s why his career is over.

And make no mistake, Rian Johnson is a complete Hack with an overinflated ego. How and why he’s even allowed to work on anything post Last Jedi just shows that Hollywood can’t learn from its own failures.

author: @ChaptermasterPedroKantor-kv5yw
text: Lucas may have cried a little over what Disney did, but I reckon 4.5 bln goes a long way to ease his pain. Also, he NEVER really cared about SW the way we did. He made a movie, it blew up, it was the one thing in his life that dominated everything, I don't think it was the thing that he felt was the most important thing to him. To his credit he ran with it and tried to steer it in a coherent way and direction, even though it wasn't always the direction we wanted. But he moved on, and so should we. It is now a Disney sh1t sandwich and it will only get worse.

text: Here's the thing though, dude. I never believed that and saw it as a social disease. I remember all the flack I got for saying Force Awakens just outright sucked when everyone was still high on their new false hope...

author: @Aikurisu
text:
Don't care.  George is much to blame for his legacy fading into obscurity in the first place.  After all, who did put folks like Kennedy and Filoni in charge of it?

Sure, it could be argued the franchise would have been in a better place if Genndy Tartakovsky took George's offer to run the show back in the day but that's an alternate reality we'll never see.

author: @SilvidGossamer
text: There is a lot to like about _Tʜᴇ Rɪsᴇ oꜰ Sᴋʏᴡᴀʟᴋᴇʀ_ imo, but it is a hot mess. Though at least it doesn't try to dump on Star Wars characters or lore per se, at least not to the degree or consistency that _Tʜᴇ Lᴀsᴛ Jᴇᴅɪ_ did. Although I do agree _TLJ_ is a better looking film than _TRoS_ in terms of sheer cinematography/artistic vision.
